pages.title: Torque Unit Conversion
pages.summary: Quick reference for converting between Nm, kgf·m, ft·lb, and in·lb.
pages.content:
Torque Conversion Chart

Nmkgf·mft·lbin·lb
10.100.748.85
50.513.6944.3
101.027.3888.5
202.0414.8177
303.0622.1266
505.1036.9443
10010.273.8885

Formula: Nm × 0.7376 = ft·lb | Nm × 8.851 = in·lb | Nm × 0.10197 = kgf·m

pages.title: Motorcycle Oil Grades Explained
pages.summary: Understanding SAE viscosity ratings, JASO MA/MA2, and API classifications.
pages.content:
SAE Viscosity

The first number (e.g. 10W-40) indicates cold-start viscosity. The W means Winter. Lower numbers flow better in cold conditions.

The second number (e.g. 10W-40) indicates viscosity at operating temperature. Higher numbers provide thicker protection at high heat.

JASO Certification

  • JASO MA — Standard for wet-clutch motorcycles. No friction modifiers that cause clutch slip.
  • JASO MA2 — Higher friction performance for modern bikes. Preferred for most current motorcycles.
  • JASO MB — For scooters with CVT. Not suitable for manual-clutch bikes.

API Rating

Look for API SG or higher. Avoid automotive oils with energy-conserving labels — they contain friction modifiers harmful to wet clutches.

pages.title: Contributing to MotoX Wiki
pages.summary: How to add or update motorcycle specifications.
pages.content:
How to Contribute

MotoX Wiki is community-maintained. You can contribute by:

  1. Forking the repository on GitHub
  2. Editing the JSON files in data/ (motorcycles, pages, settings)
  3. Committing changes and opening a pull request

Data Guidelines

  • Always cite the source (service manual, OEM documentation)
  • Include year ranges when specs vary by model year
  • Use standard units (Nm for torque, L for volume) with conversions in parentheses
  • Mark unverified data in the notes field
